translations.code.autofix.confidenceReasonFailed: {n} verify gate(s) reported failures — see {gate} below for details.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceReasonSkipped: Verify gates were skipped ({list}) — patch was not actually validated.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceReasonTier1: Deterministic transform ({category}) and {n} gate(s) passed — mechanical change, safe to PR.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceReasonTier2: AI-generated patch and {n} gate(s) passed — code-correctness still needs human review.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceTipHigh: All verify gates passed — patch is ready to PR.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceTipMedium: One or more verify gates did not pass — review carefully.
translations.code.autofix.disclaimer: AI-assisted patch. Review before merging — the engine verified lint, tests, and runner gates passed, but human judgement is required for code-correctness.
translations.code.autofix.disclaimerSAST: AI-generated patch — code correctness requires human review. Verify gates only check that lint+tests pass, not whether the change preserves intent.
translations.code.autofix.empty: No AutoFix-eligible findings yet — the inventory populates after the AutoFix system detects fixable code. Click Run on any category tab to seed this list.
translations.code.autofix.fetchError: Could not load this finding — the engine returned an error. The finding may have been resolved out of the latest scan, or the cache is out of sync. Re-running the scan often clears this.
translations.code.autofix.loadErrorHint: Most often this means the engine is running an older binary. Rebuild + restart flyto-engine; the new /autofix/findings route + autofix_finding table land on first start. Check the engine logs for the exact failure if this persists.
translations.code.autofix.noChange: No textual change between before and after.
translations.code.autofix.noMatch: No findings match the current filter.
translations.code.autofix.noPreview: No diff available — the patch generation failed or the finding is no longer detected.
translations.code.autofix.prBlockedSkipped: PR blocked — verify gates were skipped ({list}). Re-run with the runner online so the patch can be verified before merging.
translations.code.autofix.prDisabledTip: PR is gated on verify pass — see the message above for which gate failed.
translations.code.autofix.previewGenerating: Generating preview — cloning repo, running detect+transform, executing verify gates…
translations.code.autofix.subtitle: AI-powered fix suggestions for dependencies, code, and infrastructure
translations.code.autofix.summaryCount: {count} fixable findings across {groups} rule × repo groups
translations.code.autofix.tabTip: AutoFix-eligible findings — click to see per-repo fix previews and open draft PRs.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditEmpty: No runs yet — trigger AutoFix or wait for next scan to populate.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditNoGates: No gate detail recorded for this run.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditPickRun: Pick a run on the left to inspect its gate verdicts.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.categoryEmpty: No rules in this category yet — Tier 2 (AI-proposed) patches will land here as we wire more finding types.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.cleanRule: ✓ no findings — codebase clean for this rule
translations.code.autofix.warroom.hint: Tier 1 = deterministic rules (hand-coded, stable). Tier 2 = AI-proposed patches gated by lint + test + flyto-runner closed-loop probe. Tier 3 = patterns mined from repeated AI fixes get promoted into Tier 1 with human review. Every patch passes 4 verify gates before any PR is opened.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.noRules: No rules configured yet. Run a scan to populate.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.openPRTip: When enabled, every rule with at least one verified-passing patch will get a draft PR opened on GitHub.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.pentestEmpty: No Tier 1 rules for pentest findings yet — Red Team campaign results land in Security → Red Team.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionApproveTip: Tag this shape so an engineer knows to write a Tier 1 deterministic rule for it.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionApproved: Approved — ready for engineer to write Tier 1 rule
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionEmpty: No promotion candidates yet — Tier 2 needs to ship a few merged AI patches first for the miner to find patterns.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionHint: Tier 2 (AI-proposed) patches that share the same shape across ≥ 5 occurrences and ≥ 3 distinct repos in the last 30 days. A human reviews each candidate and decides whether to write a Tier 1 deterministic rule for it.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionRejectTip: Drop this shape from the queue — Tier 2 AI fallback continues to handle these.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.settingsHint: Disabled rules are skipped at scan time. Auto-merge means: when ALL 4 verify gates pass, the engine opens a draft PR without waiting for the user to click Run. Off by default — solo founders typically want to read the diff first.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.title: AutoFix — Tier 1 / 2 / 3 pipeline

translations.code.dast.apiDiscoveryEmpty: Run discovery to scan for API endpoints, documentation, and frameworks.
translations.code.dast.clickjackingDesc: Without X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors, attackers can embed your site in an iframe for clickjacking attacks.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoEncryptDesc: A cookie is set without the 'secure' flag, meaning it can be intercepted over HTTP connections.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoHttpDesc: A cookie without HttpOnly can be accessed by JavaScript, enabling session theft via XSS.
translations.code.dast.cookiePoisonDesc: URL parameters can set cookie values, which may be exploitable for session manipulation.
translations.code.dast.cookieSubdomainDesc: Authentication cookies scoped to parent domain can be accessed by any subdomain, increasing attack surface.
translations.code.dast.cspEvalDesc: CSP does not block eval(), setTimeout() and other functions that can execute unknown code, enabling code injection.
translations.code.dast.cspFallbackDesc: CSP does not define a fallback for a directive, causing the browser to treat that content type as unrestricted.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineCssDesc: CSP allows inline CSS, which can aid social engineering attacks by injecting misleading visual elements.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineJsDesc: CSP is configured but allows inline JavaScript, which is a common XSS attack vector.
translations.code.dast.cspNotSet: Content Security Policy (CSP) header not set
translations.code.dast.cspNotSetDesc: CSP is the first line of defense against XSS and data injection attacks. It controls which content sources are allowed to load and execute.
translations.code.dast.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? All discovery data, subdomains, and scan results will be permanently removed.
translations.code.dast.dirBrowsingDesc: Directory listings are enabled, potentially revealing hidden scripts, backup files, and sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.emptyDesc: Add your domains to discover exposed services, SSL status, DNS records, tech stacks, and potential vulnerabilities across your external perimeter.
translations.code.dast.envExposedDesc: Environment files expose infrastructure credentials, API keys, or other sensitive configuration information.
translations.code.dast.graphqlIntrospectDesc: GraphQL introspection reveals the entire API schema including all types, queries, and mutations.
translations.code.dast.graphqlWarning: Introspection is enabled — this exposes your full schema to anyone. Disable in production.
translations.code.dast.heartbleedDesc: OpenSSL Heartbeat Extension vulnerability allows remote attackers to read process memory, potentially disclosing sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSet: TLS not enforced with valid HSTS header
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSetDesc: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) forces browsers to use HTTPS only. Without it, users are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks via protocol downgrade.
translations.code.dast.httpOnlyDesc: The site responds on HTTP without redirecting to HTTPS, exposing traffic to interception and modification.
translations.code.dast.jwtInvalidDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with invalid signatures, allowing attackers to modify token payload without detection.
translations.code.dast.jwtNoneDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens signed with the 'none' algorithm, bypassing signature verification entirely.
translations.code.dast.jwtSelfSignedDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with self-signed JWK keys, allowing attackers to impersonate legitimate users.
translations.code.dast.jwtWeakDesc: The JWT token returned by your server uses a weak or known compromised secret, allowing attackers to forge tokens for unauthorized access.
translations.code.dast.linkRepoHint: Link a repository to filter API routes
translations.code.dast.noApiFindings: No API documentation, GraphQL, or JS bundle endpoints detected.
translations.code.dast.noDmarcDesc: Without DMARC, there is no policy to handle emails that fail SPF/DKIM checks, enabling phishing attacks using your domain.
translations.code.dast.noDnssecDesc: Without DNSSEC, DNS responses can be spoofed, potentially redirecting users to malicious servers.
translations.code.dast.noPageSpeed: No PageSpeed data yet. Run discovery to test.
translations.code.dast.noSniffDesc: Without 'nosniff', browsers may interpret files as a different MIME type, enabling XSS via MIME-type confusion.
translations.code.dast.noSpfDesc: Without SPF (Sender Policy Framework), attackers can send emails appearing to come from your domain (email spoofing).
translations.code.dast.notVerified: Not verified yet — run discovery to test
translations.code.dast.openPortDesc: A service port is publicly accessible that should typically be behind a firewall. Database, cache, and remote access ports exposed to the internet are high-risk attack vectors.
translations.code.dast.poweredByLeakDesc: The server exposes the framework/runtime it uses, helping attackers identify specific vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.psHintA11y: Missing alt text, contrast issues, or ARIA labels.
translations.code.dast.psHintBP: Using deprecated APIs or insecure resources.
translations.code.dast.psHintPerf: Slow loading. Optimize images, lazy-load, reduce JS bundle.
translations.code.dast.psHintSEO: Missing meta tags, no sitemap, or poor mobile usability.
translations.code.dast.serverLeak: Server leaks info via 'Server' HTTP header
translations.code.dast.serverLeakDesc: The server is exposing its software identity via the Server header. This helps attackers identify known vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.sessionCookieDesc: The session cookie is missing 'httpOnly' and 'secure' attributes, making it vulnerable to theft by third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sessionStorage: Session token found in browser storage
translations.code.dast.sessionStorageDesc: Session tokens stored in localStorage/sessionStorage have no protection against injected third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sourceCodeGitDesc: Git repository files are publicly accessible, potentially exposing source code and sensitive configuration.
translations.code.dast.sriMissingDesc: External scripts/styles loaded without integrity attributes can be tampered with if the CDN is compromised.
translations.code.dast.sslExpiryDesc: The TLS certificate is about to expire, which will break HTTPS connections and trigger browser warnings.
translations.code.dast.subtitle: Discover and monitor your external attack surface across all domains
translations.code.dast.tabNabbingDesc: Links with target='_blank' without 'noopener noreferrer' allow the target page to take control of the opener.
translations.code.dim.dns.emptyDesc: Run a domain scan to check DNS security records.
translations.code.dim.dns.fixAXFR: Restrict DNS zone transfers (AXFR) to authorized secondary DNS servers only.
translations.code.dim.dns.fixCAA: Add CAA record to restrict which CAs can issue certificates for your domain.
translations.code.dim.dns.fixDKIM: Configure DKIM in your email provider and publish the DNS TXT record.
translations.code.dim.dns.fixDMARC: Add DMARC TXT at _dmarc.yourdomain.com: v=DMARC1; p=none
translations.code.dim.dns.fixDNSSEC: Enable DNSSEC at your domain registrar. Most support one-click activation.
translations.code.dim.dns.fixSPF: Add SPF TXT record: v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all

translations.code.dim.ssl.fixCipher: Use ECDHE+AESGCM cipher suites. Disable RC4, DES, NULL, EXPORT.
translations.code.dim.ssl.fixExpiry: Renew certificates before expiry. Use Let\
translations.code.dim.ssl.fixHSTS: Add Strict-Transport-Security header. Submit to hstspreload.org after testing.
translations.code.dim.ssl.fixSelfSigned: Replace self-signed certificates with a trusted CA (Let\
translations.code.dim.ssl.fixTLS: Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 in your server config. Only allow TLS 1.2 and 1.3.

translations.code.scoring.gradeD: High risk. Significant vulnerabilities or technical debt.

translations.code.scoring.methodDesc: The overall score is a weighted average across active dimensions. Dimensions without scan data are automatically excluded — their weight is redistributed to the remaining dimensions. This ensures organizations aren’t penalized for dimensions they haven’t measured yet.

When a value reaches a sink without going through a sanitizer, it's an "unsanitized flow" — the most likely class of security bug. Click any flow below to see the source / sink expressions and call path.

translations.code.code.attackpath.readiness.tooltip: Whether red team can legally and safely test it now.

translations.code.dashboard.sectorTip: Where your overall score sits in your declared industry sector
translations.code.dashboard.codeCityHint: drag to orbit · click a building to drill in · red beacon = open critical
translations.code.dashboard.codeCityIdle: Scroll down to load city view
translations.code.dashboard.crossDimTip: For each Critical / High finding, count how many ALSO have the linked signal from another dimension. The join across code × external × pentest × CI is the integration moat.
translations.code.dashboard.scoreTrendSub: Historical score deltas across categories with audit-cycle markers
translations.code.domains.verifyingHint: A recent scan saw a different score. Holding the current value until a second scan confirms.
translations.code.domains.confirmDeleteDesc: This will remove the domain and all its discovery data. Scan history will be preserved.
translations.code.domains.label: domain data
translations.code.domains.unverifiedTip: Discovered but not confirmed by the verification engine. Open Evidence tab for per-source details.
translations.code.paths.title: Attack Paths
translations.code.paths.lede: Chains across findings the priority engine judged stackable into a real attack. Each chain ranks above its loudest member.
translations.code.paths.emptyDesc: Either the priority engine has not run yet or no findings stack into a chain. Use Recompute after the next scan to refresh.
translations.code.paths.priorityHint: Composite priority — max member + concentration bonus
translations.code.paths.drillHint: Open the CTEM Actions picker and search by domain / repo to drill into each member finding.
translations.code.paths.markMitigatedHint: Hide this chain from the picker. Auto-recompute re-emits if the underlying findings still chain.
translations.code.ctem.codeGuide: Mark Fixed transitions the finding to "pending verify" — the next repo rescan confirms (or reopens if still present). Open the Issues page for full per-finding detail.
translations.code.ctem.externalGuide: Mark Fixed transitions the finding to "pending verify" — the next external-posture refresh confirms (or reopens if still present). Open the Domain Intel page for full posture detail.
translations.code.ctem.errSession: Session expired — please refresh the page.
translations.code.ctem.errForbidden: You don't have permission to perform this action.
translations.code.ctem.errNetwork: Network error — check your connection and retry.
translations.code.ctem.errVerifyTransition: That verification step is not allowed from the current state. Try refreshing first.
translations.code.ctem.liveMarkedFixed: Finding marked as fixed — awaiting verify
translations.code.ctem.toastMarkedFixed: Marked as fixed — awaiting re-scan to confirm
translations.code.ctem.tipKev: CISA KEV — actively exploited in the wild
translations.code.ctem.tipAuthVerified: DAST with authenticated session confirmed this finding — exploitable, not just observed.
translations.code.ctem.tipActiveVerified: Active DAST template returned a positive match — finding is actively exploitable from outside.
translations.code.ctem.actionsLede: Risk-ranked findings prioritized by asset tier × exploitability × compensating controls — with closed-loop verification.
translations.code.ctem.complianceExportDesc: Each control bound to the scan that proves it, SHA-256 hashed and chained to the audit log.
translations.code.ctem.prioritiesDesc: Effective severity = base × asset tier × exploitability × (1 - mitigation). Sorted by priority score.
translations.code.ctem.noAlerts: No prioritized findings. Either the scan is fresh, all open issues are mitigated, or you have not connected any scan source yet.
translations.code.ctem.tipAffected: Same finding across multiple packages or repos — collapsed in the picker.
translations.code.ctem.noFilterMatchDesc: Adjust the filters above or clear search to see the full bench.
translations.code.ctem.detailEmptyTitle: Pick a finding to act on
translations.code.ctem.detailEmptyDesc: Select a finding from the bench on the left. The priority breakdown, owner editor, and Mark Fixed / False-positive actions will appear here.
translations.code.ctem.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, domain, category, threat actor…
translations.code.external.redactHint: Replace public-suffixed hostnames with ***.tld — useful when sharing externally. Not a security boundary; review before sending.
translations.code.findings.title: Findings
translations.code.findings.subtitle: Per-asset external observations across email auth, TLS, web app security, open ports, DNS, and threat-actor associations.
translations.code.findings.noSavedSets: No saved sets yet — set filters and click the bookmark+
translations.code.findings.empty: No findings match the selected filters.
translations.code.findings.customizeHelp: Toggle which columns appear in the table. Persists per org.
translations.code.findings.saveSetHelp: Give this filter combination a name so you can recall it later.
translations.code.findings.commentPlaceholder: Operator note — visible in each finding’s history drawer.
translations.code.findings.noChildAssets: No child assets recorded — finding is anchored on a single asset.
translations.code.findings.historyEmpty: No changes recorded yet. Future re-observations will appear here.
translations.code.mit.title: Compensating controls
translations.code.mit.lede: Declared mitigations reduce effective severity in the priority engine. Each row chips multiplicatively (defense-in-depth math) and caps at 85% — no claim alone closes a finding.
translations.code.mit.emptyDesc: Add the WAF rules, EDR signatures, patch baselines, or network segments that genuinely cover findings — the priority engine will discount matching rows accordingly.
translations.code.mit.claimedHint: Operator-declared but not independently verified. Click Verify to attach evidence.
translations.code.mit.coverageEmpty: No findings on the current bench match this selector. Check applies_to_tag for typos.
translations.code.mit.verifyHint: Attach evidence that this control is genuinely active
translations.code.mit.verifyDialogDesc: Attach evidence that this control is genuinely active. Examples: a WAF rule-replay screenshot URL, an EDR alert ID, a vendor confirmation link. The priority engine still applies the reduction either way — verification flips a UI badge so your team can triage what is still claim-only.
translations.code.monitoring.noMatch: No events match the current filters
translations.code.monitoring.noMatchHint: Reset the filters above to see the full feed.
translations.code.external.partialDataTitle: Some posture data could not load
translations.code.external.partialDataDesc: These dimensions are showing stale or empty data:
translations.code.external.partialDataHint: The rest of the page is still accurate. Retry to refresh just the failed dimensions.
translations.code.external.postureTrendHint: Score • Findings • Assets — hover to inspect any day

translations.code.external.detailEmptyDesc: 中間列點任一個網域，這裡會顯示分數、資產數、未修補問題與下一步操作。

translations.code.external.upcomingHint: computed from first_seen + sla — act before they breach
translations.code.exposure.brand.letterDisclaimer: This is a DRAFT prepared from publicly-collected evidence. Review every claim, attach proof of brand / trademark rights, and submit yourself to the relevant platform / registrar / hosting provider. Warroom does NOT file complaints on your behalf.

translations.code.fixq.clearedDesc: You resolved {n} {n, plural, one {finding} other {findings}} this session. Good run.

translations.code.pentest.verify.activeDastTip: Reproduced via Nuclei payload — high confidence.

translations.code.pentest.verify.multiTip: Confirmed by two independent intelligence sources.

translations.code.pentest.safety.approvals.title: Scan Approvals
translations.code.pentest.safety.approvals.subtitle: Active DAST and authenticated scans only run after an org admin approves. Required by every Nuclei + login-flow scan.
translations.code.pentest.safety.approvals.error: Could not load approvals — check your role permissions.
translations.code.pentest.safety.approvals.empty: No approvals on file. Active scans will need one before they can run.
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.title: Authenticated Scan Credentials
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.subtitle: Sealed at rest. The plaintext is sent once on upload, then only the DAST runner can unseal at scan time. Never displayed again.
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.empty: No credentials uploaded. Authenticated scans cover post-login flows competitors miss.
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.required: Asset and credential value are required.
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.dialogNotice: The plaintext is sent once and sealed server-side. After save it cannot be retrieved — only used to authenticate at scan time.
translations.code.pentest.safety.creds.label: Label (optional)
translations.code.pentest.safety.visual.title: Visual Phishing Reference
translations.code.pentest.safety.visual.subtitle: Upload your canonical login page once. Discovered lookalike domains get a perceptual-hash similarity score against it — high scores warrant a takedown letter.
translations.code.pentest.safety.visual.unavailable: Visual similarity service unavailable — the runner may not be deployed yet.
translations.code.pentest.safety.visual.empty: No reference yet. Without one, lookalike-domain detection runs blind.

translations.code.warroom.redTeamRunnerTip: Live runner queue — in-flight executions · remaining rate-limit tokens

translations.code.reports.subtitle: Preset and custom security reports — export as PDF, AI-polish, or build your own via the designer.
translations.code.fixPlan.errorSub: The AI analysis may have timed out. Click below to retry.
translations.code.repoDetail.scanTimeout: Scan is taking longer than expected. Results will appear when ready.

translations.code.upload.tooLarge: File is too large (max 100 MB). Try scanning a smaller repo or splitting the output.

translations.code.scoreTrends.subtitle: Org-level grade history and where you sit in your sector — aggregate signals only, no per-asset exposure.
translations.code.scoreTrends.noSectorHint: Set your industry sector in Settings → Organization to see how your score compares to peers in finance / SaaS / retail / healthcare / etc. No individual peers are named — only corpus-level P50 / P90 baselines.
translations.code.scoreTrends.empty: No grade changes in the last 90 days. The org has been stable.
translations.code.scoreTrends.sectorHint: Where your score sits in the public corpus for this industry. Aggregate percentiles only — no individual peer is named.
translations.code.scoreTrends.coverageUnavailable: Coverage data not yet published. The peer-corpus worker rebuilds nightly.
translations.code.autofix.permanentlyNoPreview: AutoFix has tried this finding 3 times without producing a usable diff.
translations.code.autofix.permanentlyNoPreview.detail: The file may have been refactored beyond AutoFix\
translations.code.autofix.cachedHint: cached result (file unchanged since last attempt)
translations.code.warroom.liveViewUnavailableHint: Browser preview unavailable — flyto-cloud WebSocket is not configured. Campaign is still running normally; this panel only shows the screenshot stream.
translations.code.issues.allClearDesc: Your open queue is empty. Snoozed + ignored + solved tabs still hold history. New findings will land here as scans complete.
translations.code.issues.packageDrawer.hint: See how this package affects the rest of the org
translations.code.issues.snoozeDesc: The finding will reappear after the selected period.
translations.code.issues.packageDrawer.label: Package aggregate
translations.code.news.label: the news feed
translations.code.news.pageSubtitle: Aggregated CVE / breach / threat-intel headlines from public RSS sources. Refreshed ~30 min server-side.
translations.code.security.label: security findings
translations.code.warroom.secNewOrgDesc: Security findings come from scans of your code. Connect a repository to start the SAST + dependency + secrets pipeline.
translations.code.warroom.secStep1: Connect a GitHub or GitLab repository
translations.code.warroom.secStep2: Wait for the first scan — usually 1-3 minutes
translations.code.verify.timeout: Verification timed out. The runner may be unavailable — please try again.
translations.code.warroom.verifyLibraryHint: Heads up: the scanner classified this repo as a library / SDK. If it IS a pure library, Dynamic probing proves nothing (no deployed URL). If you DO have this repo deployed — point target_url at the real deployment on the next step and proceed.
translations.code.settings.budget.title: Campaign Budget Policies
translations.code.settings.budget.desc: Set token usage limits for red team campaigns to control AI spending.
translations.code.bu.lede: Business units carve your org into teams / services. SLA policies, dashboards, and per-BU posture all key off these. Empty = org-wide (default).
translations.code.bu.emptyHint: Add a unit when you have multiple teams or services that need separate SLA budgets / dashboards.
translations.code.canonical.uploaded: Canonical login screenshot stored — similarity comparison enabled
translations.code.canonical.title: Visual phishing similarity
translations.code.canonical.body: Upload your real login page screenshot. The discovery pipeline already pHashes every captured lookalike screenshot — we compare them here to highlight visually-similar candidates (≥80% match). A high similarity is a triage signal, not a verdict.
translations.code.canonical.matchesEmpty: No lookalike screenshots score above the threshold yet. The discovery pipeline pHashes every capture; matches will appear here as candidates are detected.
translations.code.settings.ciGate.title: CI/CD Gate Policy
translations.code.settings.ciGate.blockOnDesc: Fail CI checks when findings at or above this severity are found.
translations.code.settings.ciGate.failOnLicenseDesc: Block CI when non-compliant licenses are detected.
translations.code.settings.ciGate.failOnSecretDesc: Block CI when exposed secrets or credentials are found.
translations.code.settings.ciGate.failOnIacDesc: Block CI when critical infrastructure-as-code misconfigurations are found.
translations.code.settings.ciGate.requireScanDesc: Block merge if no scan has been run on the PR branch.
translations.code.settings.deleteOrgDesc: Permanently delete this organization and all its data. This action cannot be undone.
translations.code.settings.members.title: Organization Members
translations.code.settings.members.noMembers: No members found from connected providers.
translations.code.settings.members.empty: Connect a source control provider to view organization members.
translations.code.approval.gateBody: Active DAST will not scan any asset without an approved row here. Approval expires in 1 week by default (max 90 days). Denying creates an audit trail; revoking by archive uses the same flow.
translations.code.approval.approvedEmpty: No active approvals — DAST runner will not produce any new findings until at least one is approved.
translations.code.approval.denyDialogDesc: Reason will be recorded on the audit trail. The requester can re-request later.
translations.code.cred.warnBody: Credentials are sealed via KMS the moment you submit. Backend never returns plaintext. The DAST worker is the only component that can decrypt. Approve the scan via Scan Approvals before authenticated DAST runs.
translations.code.cred.emptyHint: Add a cookie or bearer token for an asset you want the DAST runner to scan as a logged-in user.
translations.code.cred.addInfo: Plaintext is sealed server-side via your engine\
translations.code.cred.plaintextHelp: Will be sealed via KMS on submit. The UI clears this field immediately afterwards.
translations.code.cred.label: Label (operator-facing identifier)
translations.code.settings.schedule.autoPausedTip: This schedule was auto-paused after repeated failures. Click Resume after fixing the underlying issue (token expired, repo deleted, etc).
translations.code.settings.schedule.failingTip: Recent runs have failed. The scheduler is exponentially backing off; will auto-pause at 5 consecutive failures.
translations.code.settings.schedule.cadenceDesc: How often this kind of scan runs automatically.
translations.code.settings.schedule.runNowTip: Trigger one scan immediately. Does not affect the next scheduled run.
translations.code.settings.scanners.title: Platform scanners
translations.code.settings.scanners.subtitle: Registry-driven background scanners running across the platform. Toggle / re-schedule live (no redeploy).
translations.code.scoringConfig.title: Scoring Config
translations.code.scoringConfig.subtitle: Tune how the unified score weighs each category and confidence tier. Weights are auto-normalized server-side — they do not have to sum to 1.
translations.code.scoringConfig.warning: Custom weights affect every org member\
translations.code.scoringConfig.resetConfirm: Reset all scoring weights to defaults? This cannot be undone.
translations.code.settings.adminOnly: This section requires admin or owner permissions. Changes you attempt may be rejected by the server.
translations.code.sla.policy.lede: Per-severity SRE error budgets. Each policy declares how many SLA violations you tolerate in a window (default 90 days). When usage crosses alert_at_percent, the SLAMonitor flips to "warning"; at 100% it flips to "exhausted". Empty business unit = org-wide policy.
translations.code.sla.policy.emptyHint: Declare at least one policy to enable the SLA Monitor error-budget panel.
translations.code.settings.bitbucketDesc: Connect your Bitbucket workspace to import repositories.
translations.code.settings.events.title: System Events
translations.code.settings.events.subtitle.admin: Internal diagnostic log across ALL orgs. Use org_id filter to scope. Last 24h default.
translations.code.settings.events.subtitle.org: Internal diagnostic log for this organisation. Last 24h by default.
translations.code.settings.events.empty: No events match the current filters.
translations.code.threatIntel.iocLookupLede: Indicators of compromise — C2 / URL / IP / phishing / credential exposure.
